text: It is unlawful to refuse to rent or sell housing, make housing unavailable, or discriminate in the terms or conditions of housing based on race, color, religion, sex, familial status, national origin, or disability.
Housing providers may not treat applicants or residents differently based on protected characteristics or steer individuals toward or away from housing opportunities.
Individuals with disabilities are entitled to reasonable accommodations in housing policies, practices, or services when necessary to ensure equal opportunity to use and enjoy a dwelling.
Credit decisions must be made without discrimination based on protected characteristics, and applicants must be evaluated consistently using lawful criteria.
